name: Kuwait
intro: Kuwait's project landscape is anchored by oil-sector capital programmes and a government working through a long infrastructure backlog, from housing cities to the northern economic zone. Certified project managers are in steady demand at contractors, consultancies and ministries alike, and PMP is the credential Kuwaiti employers name most often in tenders that require qualified staff. Because the local training market is small, most professionals in Kuwait City certify through live online programmes — which is exactly what we run. Batches are taught over a single weekend on the IST schedule, converting to comfortable daytime hours in Kuwait. Instruction is in English with an international cohort, and we issue invoices suitable for employer sponsorship. After the workshop you get help with the exam application, full-length mock exams, and direct access to the trainer for questions until you pass.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Kuwait?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Kuwait?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Kuwait.
